strategic_pillars.name: Trifecta
strategic_pillars.status: achieved ahead of plan (delivered in 2024, one year early)
strategic_pillars.targets: Triple-digit Adjusted EBITDA per APCD, double-digit Adjusted EPS, and ROIC in the teens by year-end 2025; framed as the financial bridge out of the pandemic and back to investment-grade metrics.

strategic_pillars.name: Perfecta
strategic_pillars.targets: By 2027: Adjusted EPS of approximately $21 (20% CAGR off 2024 base), 17% Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion, and ROIC of 17%+; supported by new ships (Icon/Star/Utopia class), destination investments (Perfect Day Mexico, Royal Beach Club Paradise Island/Cozumel), and the loyalty/commercial platform.

recurring_headwinds.fuel: Fuel is managed through hedging and consumption optimization rather than passed through. Hedge coverage has stayed in a 54%-68% band on the current year (55% in 2022-Q1, 64% in 2022-Q3, 61% across 2023-Q4 through 2024-Q3, 66% in 2025-Q2, 68% in 2025-Q3, and ~60% for 2026 per 2026-Q1). Even with hedging, fuel and FX combined have repeatedly been called out as a $0.20+ EPS drag (e.g., -$0.21 in 2023-Q3 guidance) and were quantified at $0.62/share standalone (or $0.74 including JVs) in the 2026-Q1 outlook.
recurring_headwinds.fx: Roughly 20% of revenue is now sourced in non-USD currencies after the post-COVID shift to more North American itineraries (2022-Q3), down from ~25% pre-pandemic. Management presents nearly all yield and cost metrics on a constant-currency basis (2022-Q2 through 2023-Q2), and FX is routinely bundled with fuel and interest into the quarterly EPS guide framework. 2026-Q1 reiterates a 1.5%-2.5% constant-currency Net Yield range for the full year as the cleanest demand signal.
recurring_headwinds.dry_dock: Dry-dock cadence is structurally elevated post-COVID because ships that missed their pandemic-era windows are being re-sequenced (2023-Q2). 2024 carried roughly double the dry-dock days of 2023 and, together with the opening of Hideaway Beach, added ~300-380 bps to NCC ex-fuel (2023-Q3, 2023-Q4). Cost cadence is front-half loaded as most dry docks fall in late Q1/early Q2, with second-half relief from fewer dock days and incremental APCDs from new ships such as Utopia.
recurring_headwinds.geopolitical: The war in Ukraine was flagged immediately in 2022-Q1 (2,000+ Ukrainian crew) along with broader supply-chain pressure. Middle East exposure resurfaced in 2023-Q3 when sailings that included Israel were called out as part of a -$0.21 EPS impact alongside fuel/FX. Management consistently frames geopolitical events as itinerary-redeployment problems rather than demand problems, leveraging the global fleet to reposition capacity.
recurring_headwinds.macro: Management's standing view is that the consumer remains healthy and that cruise trades at a meaningful value gap to land-based vacations, which historically cushions demand in softer macro periods (2022-Q1). Close-in booking strength, record onboard spend and pre-cruise penetration are cited as evidence the consumer continues to prioritize experiences (reiterated through 2025-Q3 and 2026-Q1). Inflation and rate volatility are acknowledged but are typically described as managed through pricing power, the loyalty base, and the value-gap narrative rather than as demand destruction.

speaker_roles.Jason Liberty: President and CEO, Royal Caribbean Group; primary voice on strategy, Trifecta/Perfecta progress, and capital allocation.
speaker_roles.Naftali Holtz: CFO, Royal Caribbean Group; owns guidance walk, fuel/FX/interest sensitivities, leverage and buyback commentary.
speaker_roles.Michael Bayley: President and CEO, Royal Caribbean International; speaks to flagship brand demand, Perfect Day at CocoCay/Royal Beach Club, new-ship launches (Icon/Star class).
speaker_roles.Michael McCarthy: VP / Head of Investor Relations, Royal Caribbean Group (through 2024-Q2); hosted and framed the earnings calls.
speaker_roles.Blake Vanier: VP of Investor Relations, Royal Caribbean Group (Blake Bannier in 2024-Q3 transcript); current IR host from 2024-Q3 onward.
speaker_roles.Sean Wagner: AVP, Leisure Equity Research, Citigroup; recurring sell-side questioner on yield and cost cadence.
speaker_roles.Lizzie Dove: VP and Equity Research Analyst, Goldman Sachs; recurring sell-side questioner on consumer health and capital returns.

2024-Q4.notes: First full year reporting Trifecta achievement (one year ahead of plan); Perfecta program articulated.

2025-Q2.notes: Call hosted in Miami with Liberty, Holtz, and Bayley; reiterated consumer strength and forward bookings.

2025-Q3.notes: Strict one-question-per-analyst format on the call; continued strong booked position and onboard spend.

2026-Q1.quarter_highlights: Q1 revenue +11% YoY; earnings ~11% above guidance; $1.1B returned via dividends and buybacks; record Wave Season.
